title: Two-finger swipe to switch between sibling screens
design_brief: Two-finger horizontal swipe across the app body to switch between sibling tabs (alternative gesture for power users). Reference: iPad two-finger gestures, Notion mobile.
mobile_reasoning: Gesture.Pan().minPointers(2) requires two fingers. Translation derives next tab; on end, snap to nearest with spring.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const SWIPE = {threshold: 0.3};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Pan with minPointers(2).
accessibility_considerations: Standard tabs nav still available via tab bar.
performance_notes: Gesture values stay on the UI thread through shared values; React state is not updated per frame.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Multi-touch gesture. 2) Power-user shortcut without conflicting with content.

title: Bottom sheet with floating CTA pinned to bottom edge
design_brief: A bottom sheet with scrollable content + a 'Confirm' CTA pinned to the bottom that stays visible regardless of scroll. Reference: Stripe checkout sheet, Apple Pay confirmation.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: CTA without subtle separator from content
mobile_reasoning: Sheet has flex column: ScrollView { flex 1 } then CTA bar { fixed }. Safe-area-padding bottom on CTA.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Backdrop opacity derives from sheet translation, keeping dimming and position synchronized.
gesture_strategy: Inherit from rn-002.
accessibility_considerations: Sheet uses accessibilityViewIsModal and includes an explicit close action.
performance_notes: Translation and backdrop opacity are derived on the UI thread with one shared value.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Safe area respected. 2) Persistent CTA matches Stripe/Apple Pay pattern.

title: Search-as-you-type list with debounced fetch and result fade-in
design_brief: A search results screen: type query → after 200ms debounce, fetch results → results fade-in. Old results crossfade to new. Reference: Spotify search, Apple Mail search.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hard swap of old → new results
Empty state with no skeleton during fetch
mobile_reasoning: Debounced query via useDeferredValue + setTimeout. Old results dim during fetch; new results fade in.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const DEBOUNCE = 200;
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Single timer per query change. FlashList for results.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Debounced UX standard. 2) Worklet opacity. 3) FlashList recycling.

title: Combobox: input with dropdown autocomplete
design_brief: A text input that shows a dropdown list of suggestions as user types (city autocomplete, recipient search). Reference: Apple Maps search, iMessage recipient picker.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Dropdown that pushes content (use absolute overlay)
mobile_reasoning: Input + suggestion list absolutely positioned below. List filters as text changes. Tap suggestion fills input.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const COMBO = {maxHeight: 240};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Width matches input.

gesture_strategy: None custom.
interaction_details: Tap suggestion → fills input, closes dropdown
accessibility_considerations: Each input has a label, error hint, visible validation copy, and disabled submit state.
performance_notes: Filter on render — acceptable for small option lists.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Touch-tap selection. 2) Auto-hiding dropdown.

title: Bottom tabs with hidden tab on certain screens
design_brief: Tab bar visible on root screens but hidden on detail screens (chat detail, story viewer). Reference: WhatsApp chat detail hides tabs, Instagram story.
mobile_reasoning: Expo Router screen option tabBarStyle: {display: 'none'} on detail screens. Or animate tab bar Y with shared value tied to navigation focus.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Detail screen mounts: tab bar hides
Back: tab bar reappears (native nav handles)
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native tab bar control. 2) Cleanup on unmount.

title: Onboarding pagination dots with active state
design_brief: An onboarding screen with 3-5 pages and pagination dots at bottom that show progress. Active dot is wider/longer. Reference: Apple Watch first-launch, Headspace onboarding.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Dots fixed position when number > 5 (use scroll indicator)
mobile_reasoning: Each dot width animates between small (8) and wide (24) based on active index. Single shared value drives multiple derived widths.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const DOT = {sm: 8, lg: 24, gap: 6};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Per-dot width derived from active value.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hooks are declared once at component top level; mapped marks are plain memoized data, so the Rules of Hooks are preserved.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Animation communicates 'you are here'. 2) Spring physics.

title: Animated heart-rate ECG line
design_brief: A health app showing an animated ECG-style heartbeat trace. Reference: Apple Health, Fitbit heart rate.
mobile_reasoning: Skia path with a sliding window of pulse waveform. Frame callback advances the window.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const ECG = {speed: 60, color: '#FF3B5C'};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Touch is optional; the drawing exposes a semantic image label and avoids gesture-only meaning.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: 200 samples per frame is cheap.
what_makes_this_native: 1) GPU path rendering. 2) Frame callback at 60fps. 3) Worklet math.

title: Color picker grid with HSL slider
design_brief: A color picker: 5x5 swatch grid for quick picks + lightness slider. Reference: iOS Notes color picker, Bear note tag color.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Complex color wheel (overkill for most apps)
mobile_reasoning: Pre-defined HSL swatches. Selected swatch has ring around it. Lightness slider adjusts L on selected hue.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const PALETTE = {rows: 5, cols: 5, swatch: 36};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Tap.
accessibility_considerations: Each swatch accessibilityLabel with hsl values.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Touch-tap selection. 2) Haptic feedback.

title: Photo editor filters strip
design_brief: A horizontal strip of filter thumbnails below an image (Instagram-style): tap a filter to apply. Reference: Instagram filter strip, VSCO.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Re-rendering full image on each filter tap
mobile_reasoning: Use Skia's ColorMatrix to apply filters on the fly. Each filter is a 4×5 matrix. Thumbnails show the matrix applied to a low-res preview.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const FILTER_THUMB = 64;
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Tap.
accessibility_considerations: Picker, queue status, and failed upload states are announced with labels and live status text.
performance_notes: Skia ColorMatrix is GPU. Thumbnails small.
what_makes_this_native: 1) GPU color matrix. 2) Skia image filters.

title: Dark academic: parchment background, serif type, soft amber accents
design_brief: A reading/note app aesthetic: warm parchment-yellow background, classic serif type, soft brown shadows, amber accent. Reference: Day One in vintage theme, Bear notes.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Cold gray dark mode masquerading as 'classic'
Vibrant accents that fight the muted palette
mobile_reasoning: Custom palette + serif font. Subtle paper texture optional via Skia noise.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const DARK_ACADEMIC = {bg:'#1A140E', card:'#241C12', text:'#E8D8B8', accent:'#D4A156', muted:'#9C8866', font:'CrimsonPro-Regular', fontBold:'CrimsonPro-Bold'};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Wide margins.

gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Contrast ratio meets AA: parchment vs dark.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Custom serif on mobile. 2) Warm palette defies the default 'cool dark'.

title: useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations
design_brief: A list with a filter input. When typing, the list recomputes from a large dataset. Using useDeferredValue lets React keep the input responsive while the list updates async.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Synchronous heavy filter on every keystroke
Blocking the UI thread (the JS thread really)
mobile_reasoning: useDeferredValue marks the filtered query as low-priority — React renders the input change immediately and recomputes the list when idle.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Typing stays responsive even with 10000 items
List shows stale state during transitions
accessibility_considerations: isStale could announce 'loading' to screen readers.
performance_notes: React 18+ deferred work. Combined with FlashList recycling, sustains 60fps.
what_makes_this_native: 1) React 18 concurrent features in RN. 2) FlashList partnership.

title: Switch and toggle with proper accessibility semantics
design_brief: Toggle controls that VoiceOver/TalkBack announce correctly: state, role, value text. Reference: iOS Settings switches.
mobile_reasoning: accessibilityRole='switch' + accessibilityState={{checked}} is the contract VoiceOver expects.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Tap.
interaction_details: Tap on row or on switch toggles
accessibility_considerations: This task IS the consideration. role='switch' is correct.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Role='switch' is honored by both VoiceOver and TalkBack. 2) Native Switch component.

title: Auto-scrolling marquee text for overflow headlines
design_brief: News ticker or now-playing track where title is too long to fit: scrolls horizontally on a loop. Reference: now-playing on lock screens, news tickers.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Truncation when scrolling is more informative
Scrolling instantly without pause at end
mobile_reasoning: Measure text width; if greater than container, animate translateX in a loop with pause at start/end.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const MARQ = {pauseMs: 1500, speed: 30};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: If text overflows: scroll left, pause, scroll back
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Worklet-driven scroll. 2) Auto-measure with conditional behavior.

title: Heat map overlay on react-native-maps
design_brief: Display a heat map of activity density. Reference: Strava heatmap, weather radar overlays.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Rendering thousands of circles as Markers
Custom Skia overlay that doesn't pan with map
mobile_reasoning: react-native-maps has Heatmap on iOS via PROVIDER_DEFAULT. On Android with Google provider, also supported. Pass weighted points.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: Provider differs but Heatmap component handles.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Camera animation is short and deterministic; selection sheet appears without re-laying out the map.
gesture_strategy: Marker taps move the camera and open a native detail sheet; the same action is available from the result list.
accessibility_considerations: Markers have titles and descriptions, and the selected place is repeated in a readable detail sheet.
performance_notes: Map camera changes are imperative through refs; marker state is small and does not rerender tiles.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native Heatmap rendering via MapKit/Google Maps.

title: Donut chart with animated segments
design_brief: A donut chart breaking down spending/time categories. Segments animate in sequentially. Reference: Apple Health activity rings, budgeting apps.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: All segments animate at once (no sequence)
mobile_reasoning: Skia paths per segment. Each segment's sweep angle animates with stagger.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const DONUT = {stroke: 32};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Diameter from prop.

gesture_strategy: Tap or focus a data mark to expose the exact value; the semantic legend mirrors the same interaction for screen readers.
interaction_details: Segments draw in sequence with stagger
accessibility_considerations: Legend list provides semantic data to VoiceOver.
performance_notes: Geometry is memoized and Skia renders marks on the native canvas, avoiding React work during interaction.
what_makes_this_native: 1) GPU arc paths. 2) Staggered reveal.

title: Pull-down menu (iOS 14+) and overflow menu (Android)
design_brief: A header overflow menu. iOS: pull-down UIMenu from a button (introduced iOS 14). Android: vertical dots opening a popup. Reference: Mail iOS pull-down, any Android app overflow.
mobile_reasoning: Expo Router header right + per-platform implementation. iOS uses MenuView from @react-native-menu/menu (which wraps UIMenu). Android uses a custom popup or the same library on Android.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: This task IS the difference.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Native.
interaction_details: iOS: tap → native UIMenu drops down with haptic
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) UIMenu on iOS — system component. 2) Material popup on Android.

title: Tab bar with custom haptic on selection
design_brief: Tab bar where each tab fires haptic per platform convention. Reference: most well-tuned apps.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Heavy haptic on tab change (too much)
Same haptic on already-active tab tap (use stronger one for 'scroll to top')
mobile_reasoning: tabPress event listener fires haptic. If user taps already-active tab, treat as 'scroll to top' with different haptic.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Already-active double tap could scroll to top with medium haptic
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Per-tap haptic. 2) Convention-respecting feedback.

title: Tag input field: chips with add/remove
design_brief: A field where user types a tag and presses comma/enter to add as a chip. Chips have delete button. Reference: Apple Mail recipients, Gmail label add.
mobile_reasoning: Wrap component: TextInput + Pressable chips in flex-wrap row. onSubmitEditing or comma-key adds chip.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Chips wrap.

gesture_strategy: Tap targets are at least 44px and form submission is explicit; no hidden gesture is required.
interaction_details: Type + comma or enter: chip added with spring
accessibility_considerations: Each input has a label, error hint, visible validation copy, and disabled submit state.
performance_notes: Hooks are declared once at component top level; mapped marks are plain memoized data, so the Rules of Hooks are preserved.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Direct touch chip removal. 2) Haptic per add/remove.

title: Sheet that snaps to content height (auto-size)
design_brief: A sheet whose first snap is the height of its content (not a hardcoded fraction). Reference: Apple AirDrop sheet, Cash App confirmations.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hardcoded snap that's wrong for short content
Sheet too tall for short content (lots of empty space)
mobile_reasoning: Measure content via onLayout; set snap to measured height + safe area + padding.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Header pan controls the sheet; close remains available as an explicit button for non-gesture users.
accessibility_considerations: Sheet uses accessibilityViewIsModal and includes an explicit close action.
performance_notes: Translation and backdrop opacity are derived on the UI thread with one shared value.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Bottom-anchored — sheet feels rooted. 2) Auto-fits content.

title: FlashList with sticky and inverted (chat-style)
design_brief: A chat list inverted (newest at bottom), with a sticky date separator that updates as user scrolls. Reference: iMessage, WhatsApp, Telegram.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Sticky header that re-renders on every scroll frame
mobile_reasoning: FlashList supports inverted via inverted prop. Sticky date is rendered separately, tracking the topmost visible cell's date via onViewableItemsChanged.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Date pill at top updates as user scrolls back in time
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: FlashList uses stable keys, memoized rows, and estimated sizes to keep scrolling predictable.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native inverted scrolling. 2) Viewability tracking for sticky.

title: Smart pull-to-refresh icon that pre-rotates as you pull
design_brief: A custom pull-to-refresh where the refresh icon rotates proportionally to pull distance (not just at the end). Pre-communicates 'releasing now will trigger refresh'. Reference: Apollo for Reddit, various polished apps.
mobile_reasoning: Pull distance → icon rotation interpolation. Past threshold, icon locks in 'ready' position. Release triggers actual refresh.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const ROT = {threshold: 80};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Inherited from native scroll.
interaction_details: Pull: icon fades in and rotates
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Proportional gesture feedback. 2) UI-thread interpolation.

title: PDF viewer with pinch-zoom and page swipe
design_brief: A PDF reader: pinch to zoom, swipe to next page, page-number indicator. Reference: Apple Books PDF, Acrobat Reader mobile.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Pretending PDFs are web (use native renderer)
mobile_reasoning: react-native-pdf or expo-asset + native PDF rendering. Wrap in pinch-zoom from rn-025.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Inherits.
interaction_details: Pinch zoom built into native PDF
accessibility_considerations: PDF a11y depends on the file.
performance_notes: Uploads stream progress per item and keep large media out of React state except URI/status metadata.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native PDF rendering. 2) Native paging.

title: Skeuomorphic notebook: paper texture and ruled lines
design_brief: A notes app skinned as a ruled notebook page with paper texture. Reference: classic Notes (pre-iOS 7), some indie writing apps.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Lines that don't align with text baseline
No paper texture (flat = breaks the illusion)
mobile_reasoning: Background image of paper. Ruled lines drawn with Skia as horizontal lines at line-height intervals. Text rendered above with same line-height so it sits on the lines.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const NB = {lineH: 28};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Skia underlay for ruled lines. 2) Aligned with text baseline.

title: Hold-to-record voice with slide-to-cancel
design_brief: WhatsApp-style voice recording: hold mic button to record, slide left past threshold to cancel. Reference: WhatsApp, Telegram, iMessage voice memo.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Cancel requiring exact tap (use generous slide threshold)
mobile_reasoning: Pan-while-held tracks finger; if x crosses cancel threshold, stop recording and discard.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const CANCEL_X = -120;
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Mic translates with finger; cancel indicator fades in.
gesture_strategy: LongPress + Pan.
interaction_details: Slide left: mic translates + cancel hint fades in
Release before threshold: selection haptic + commit
accessibility_considerations: accessibilityLabel='Hold to record, slide left to cancel'.
performance_notes: Gesture values stay on the UI thread through shared values; React state is not updated per frame.
what_makes_this_native: 1) WhatsApp's exact pattern. 2) Hold-while-drag gesture. 3) Per-state haptics.

title: Lottie alternative: Skia-based custom loading
design_brief: A custom loading indicator built with Skia (no Lottie dependency). Three orbiting dots around a center. Reference: bespoke loading states.
mobile_reasoning: Skia draws three circles; angle derived from time.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const ORBIT = {radius: 14, dotSize: 6};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Custom loader without library bloat. 2) GPU rendering.

title: Haptic feedback vocabulary: knowing when to use which
design_brief: A reference pattern for haptic usage. Selection for toggles/selections, Impact (Light/Medium/Heavy) for press/commit/significant action, Notification (Success/Warning/Error) for outcomes.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Same haptic for success and error
mobile_reasoning: expo-haptics maps to native UIImpactFeedbackGenerator / VibrationEffect.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: Android haptic feel varies by OEM.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Selection: scroll picker, toggle, scrub crossings
Heavy impact: significant moments (camera shutter, success)
accessibility_considerations: Haptics aid blind users — never the only feedback channel.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Mobile-only sensory channel. 2) Vocabulary distinct from visual UI.

title: Time-of-day picker (analog clock face)
design_brief: A clock-face time picker like Google Material's: tap a number to set the hour, drag the hand to fine-tune. Reference: Material 3 time picker, Things 3 due time.
mobile_reasoning: Skia draws clock face. Hand is a Skia line whose angle is shared value. Drag gesture computes angle from finger position relative to center.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const CLOCK = {size: 240};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Pan from center.
accessibility_considerations: Each input has a label, error hint, visible validation copy, and disabled submit state.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Direct touch input. 2) Skia drawing. 3) Snap with spring.

title: File picker with multi-select and type filter
design_brief: Pick documents (PDF, images, etc) from device. Multi-select. Show preview thumbnails. Reference: iOS Files picker, Android document picker.
mobile_reasoning: expo-document-picker exposes both single and multi-select with type filter.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: Native pickers per platform.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Progress changes animate through native image opacity or progress width while upload state remains resumable.
gesture_strategy: Tap to pick or capture media; long running upload work is represented as cancellable queue state instead of blocking gestures.
interaction_details: Returns assets with uri, name, size, mimeType
accessibility_considerations: Picker, queue status, and failed upload states are announced with labels and live status text.
performance_notes: Uploads stream progress per item and keep large media out of React state except URI/status metadata.
what_makes_this_native: 1) System document picker. 2) Multi-select native. 3) Type filtering via MIME.

title: Magazine cover layout: full-bleed image, mixed type sizes
design_brief: A magazine-cover-style detail screen: full-bleed image, title overlay in giant condensed type, subtitle in smaller body. Reference: Apple Music Editorial, Spotify playlists.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Type that doesn't sit on the image
No text shadow / gradient overlay for legibility
mobile_reasoning: Image + gradient overlay at bottom for text legibility. Condensed display font for title.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: const MAG = {titleFont: 'Inter-Black', titleSize: 56};
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

animation_strategy: Motion is bounded, cancellable, and tied to state changes the user can understand.
gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
interaction_details: Gradient at bottom for text contrast
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Full-bleed (no safe area top by intent). 2) Gradient ensures text contrast. 3) Display font.

title: Suspending offscreen content via Suspense + lazy
design_brief: A heavy detail screen with many sub-sections. Below-the-fold sections load via Suspense as user scrolls toward them. Reference: any app with progressively-rendered detail.
mobile_reasoning: Each section wrapped in Suspense with a lazy import. As user scrolls, sections come into viewport and lazily mount. Combined with InteractionManager for initial paint.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: Component-level sizing, spacing, and hit-target constants are named instead of hidden in inline styles.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: iOS and Android keep the same behavior; platform-specific shadow/elevation or picker APIs are isolated at the edge.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: Layout uses available width, safe-area padding, and minimum 44px targets so phone and tablet density stay usable.

gesture_strategy: Primary action is exposed through a Pressable with an accessibility action, with gestures treated as acceleration only.
accessibility_considerations: Every primary operation has a visible label, 44px target, and non-gesture path.
performance_notes: Hot-path work is kept outside render loops; state updates happen only on committed user-visible changes.
what_makes_this_native: 1) React 18 Suspense in RN. 2) Layered with Skia skeletons. 3) Reduces initial JS payload.
